questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to come to a full stop and wait until the signal turns green before proceeding.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal means you should:
choices: Stop, yield, and proceed when it is safe
Continue without slowing
Treat it as a green light
Stop and wait for it to turn green
questions.explanation: A flashing red light works like a stop sign: you must stop, yield to other traffic, and go only when it is safe.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light indicate?
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
It is safe to speed through the intersection
The light is about to turn green
You must always come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that the signal will soon turn red and you should stop if you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: What shape is a STOP sign?
choices: Octagon
Triangle
Diamond
Rectangle
questions.explanation: The STOP sign has a unique eight-sided octagonal shape so it can be recognized even when partly covered.

questions.prompt: A yellow diamond-shaped sign is used to:
choices: Warn of a hazard or condition ahead
Show a speed limit
Mark a school zone only
Give directions to a city
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs are warning signs that alert drivers to hazards or changing road conditions ahead.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center of the road means:
choices: You may not cross it to pass
You may pass whenever traffic is light
You may park on the line
The road ends ahead
questions.explanation: A solid yellow centerline on your side means passing is prohibited because it is unsafe to cross there.

questions.prompt: Orange traffic signs generally indicate:
choices: Construction or maintenance work zones
School crossings
No parking areas
Hospital locations
questions.explanation: Orange signs mark construction and maintenance areas where you should slow down and watch for workers.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop completely before proceeding
The intersection is closed
Make a U-turn
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light tells you to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: A red and white triangular sign with the word YIELD means:
choices: Let other traffic go first
Come to a complete stop every time
No entry allowed
Merge faster than other traffic
questions.explanation: A YIELD sign requires you to slow down and give the right of way to other traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: White lines on the pavement are used to:
choices: Separate lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
Separate traffic moving in opposite directions
Mark the edge of a parking lot only
Indicate a bicycle lane only
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes traveling in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, if two vehicles arrive at the same time, who goes first?
choices: The driver on the right
The driver on the left
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are turning left at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians
Only vehicles behind you
No one, since you are turning
Only large trucks
questions.explanation: A driver turning left must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the road.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian is in a crosswalk, you should:
choices: Stop and let the pedestrian cross
Honk so they hurry
Drive around them quickly
Proceed because vehicles have the right of way
questions.explanation: Pedestrians in a crosswalk have the right of way, and drivers must stop to let them cross safely.

questions.prompt: In Arizona, a right turn on a red light is:
choices: Allowed after a complete stop unless a sign prohibits it
Never allowed
Allowed without stopping
Allowed only at night
questions.explanation: You may turn right on red after stopping completely and yielding, unless a posted sign prohibits the turn.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
Speed up to get out of the area
Stop immediately in your lane
Turn left in front of it
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right and stopping until they have passed.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
Stop fully even if it is clear
Enter quickly to merge
Have the right of way over circulating traffic
questions.explanation: Drivers entering a roundabout must yield to vehicles already circulating inside it.

questions.prompt: A left turn on a red light is permitted in Arizona only when:
choices: Turning from a one-way street onto another one-way street
There is no oncoming traffic
You are in a hurry
It is after dark
questions.explanation: Arizona allows a left turn on red only from a one-way street onto another one-way street after stopping.

questions.prompt: Before making a turn, you should signal:
choices: Well in advance of the turn
Only if other cars are nearby
After you begin turning
Only when turning left
questions.explanation: Signaling well before a turn warns other drivers and pedestrians of your intentions.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection where you and another car arrive about the same time, you should:
choices: Yield to the vehicle on your right
Always go first
Yield to the vehicle on your left
Honk and proceed
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, yield to the vehicle approaching from your right when arrivals are simultaneous.

questions.prompt: When making a right turn, you should generally turn into:
choices: The lane closest to the right curb
The far left lane
Any open lane
The center turn lane
questions.explanation: Right turns should be made from and into the lane nearest the right-hand curb to keep traffic orderly.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in an Arizona residential or business district is:
choices: 25 mph
15 mph
35 mph
45 mph
questions.explanation: Arizona sets a prima facie limit of 25 mph in residential and business districts unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, the speed limit in a school crossing zone in Arizona is:
choices: 15 mph
25 mph
10 mph
30 mph
questions.explanation: The prima facie speed limit when approaching a school crossing is 15 mph to protect children.

questions.prompt: The maximum speed limit on a rural interstate highway in Arizona is typically:
choices: 75 mph
65 mph
55 mph
85 mph
questions.explanation: Arizona allows up to 75 mph on rural interstate highways unless a lower limit is posted.

questions.prompt: The maximum speed limit on an urban-area highway in Arizona is typically:
choices: 65 mph
75 mph
55 mph
45 mph
questions.explanation: Highways in urban areas of Arizona generally have a maximum limit of 65 mph unless otherwise posted.

questions.prompt: A good rule for following distance in normal conditions is at least:
choices: Three seconds behind the vehicle ahead
One second behind the vehicle ahead
One car length at any speed
Ten feet behind the vehicle ahead
questions.explanation: Keeping at least a three-second following distance gives you time to react to sudden stops.

questions.prompt: Even when a speed limit is posted, Arizona law requires you to:
choices: Drive at a speed that is reasonable and prudent for conditions
Always drive exactly at the limit
Drive faster to keep traffic moving
Match the speed of the slowest vehicle
questions.explanation: You must always drive at a speed that is reasonable and prudent for the current road and weather conditions.

questions.prompt: When driving in rain or heavy traffic, you should:
choices: Increase your following distance
Decrease your following distance
Keep the same following distance
Tailgate to stay dry
questions.explanation: Poor conditions and heavy traffic require a larger following distance because stopping takes longer.

questions.prompt: How does higher speed affect your stopping distance?
choices: It increases the distance needed to stop
It decreases the distance needed to stop
It has no effect on stopping
It only affects braking on hills
questions.explanation: The faster you drive, the longer it takes and the farther you travel before your vehicle can stop.

questions.prompt: You should slow down before entering a curve because:
choices: Braking hard in a curve can cause loss of control
Curves have higher speed limits
It is illegal to brake before a curve
Slowing in a curve is impossible
questions.explanation: Reducing speed before a curve helps maintain traction, since hard braking in the curve can cause skidding.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it:
choices: Can block traffic and cause crashes
Always improves safety
Saves the most fuel
Is required in all lanes
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly can impede the normal flow of traffic and lead to dangerous situations.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, slower traffic should generally:
choices: Keep to the right
Stay in the left lane
Drive in the center lane only
Change lanes frequently
questions.explanation: Slower vehicles should keep right so faster traffic can use the left lanes to pass.

questions.prompt: When passing another vehicle, you should normally pass:
choices: On the left
On the right shoulder
On either side equally
Only at intersections
questions.explanation: Passing is normally done on the left when the lane is clear and it is legal and safe.

questions.prompt: You should NOT pass another vehicle when:
choices: You are near a curve or hilltop and cannot see far ahead
The road is straight and clear
There is a broken yellow line on your side
Traffic is light
questions.explanation: Passing near curves or hills is dangerous because you cannot see oncoming traffic in time.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rear-view mirror
Your front bumper clears it
You are alongside it
The other driver signals you
questions.explanation: Returning to your lane only after seeing the passed vehicle in your mirror ensures you have enough room.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow centerline means:
choices: Passing is allowed when it is safe
Passing is never allowed
The road is one-way
You must stop ahead
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line indicates that passing is permitted when the way ahead is clear and safe.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder for blind spots
Only check your rear-view mirror
Speed up and merge
Signal after you move over
questions.explanation: Checking mirrors and blind spots while signaling helps you change lanes safely.

questions.prompt: If another driver is passing you, you should:
choices: Maintain or slightly reduce your speed
Speed up to prevent the pass
Move into their lane
Brake suddenly
questions.explanation: Letting a passing driver complete the maneuver by not speeding up keeps both vehicles safe.

questions.prompt: When may you legally exceed the speed limit to pass another vehicle?
choices: Never
When the road is clear
On the highway only
When you are running late
questions.explanation: You may never exceed the posted speed limit, even to complete a pass.

questions.prompt: On a road with multiple lanes in your direction, the left lane is generally used for:
choices: Passing
Slow traffic
Parking
Turning right
questions.explanation: The left lane on a multilane road is generally reserved for passing while slower traffic keeps right.

questions.prompt: Arizona's Move Over law requires you to move over or slow down for:
choices: Any stopped vehicle displaying flashing lights
Only police cars
Only ambulances
Only tow trucks
questions.explanation: The Move Over law applies to any stopped vehicle showing flashing lights, including police, fire, tow, and other vehicles.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, you should:
choices: Allow extra following distance
Follow more closely than a car
Pass in the same lane
Use your high beams
questions.explanation: Motorcycles can stop quickly, so leaving extra following distance gives you time to react.

questions.prompt: Why are large trucks dangerous to follow too closely?
choices: They have large blind spots and need long stopping distances
They always brake faster than cars
They cannot turn
They have no mirrors
questions.explanation: Trucks have deep blind spots and need far more distance to stop than passenger cars.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Give them room when passing
Pass as closely as possible
Honk to move them off the road
Ignore them at intersections
questions.explanation: Bicyclists are vulnerable, so give them space when passing and watch for them at intersections.

questions.prompt: When you cannot safely change lanes for a stopped emergency vehicle, the Move Over law requires you to:
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop in your lane
Maintain your speed
Make a U-turn
questions.explanation: If you cannot move over safely, you must slow down and pass with caution.

questions.prompt: When changing lanes, why should you check carefully for motorcycles?
choices: They are smaller and easy to miss in blind spots
They are always in the left lane
They cannot stop quickly
They have the same blind spots as trucks
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are small and can be hidden in blind spots, so check carefully before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: When driving near a large truck, you should avoid:
choices: Cutting in front and braking suddenly
Passing it on the left when clear
Leaving extra space
Signaling your moves
questions.explanation: Cutting in front of a truck and braking is dangerous because trucks need a long distance to stop.

questions.prompt: How much space should you give a motorcycle in traffic?
choices: A full lane width
Half a lane
Just enough to share the lane
No special space
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane, so do not share a lane or crowd them.

questions.prompt: Where should you watch most carefully for pedestrians?
choices: At intersections and crosswalks
Only on highways
Only in parking lots
Only at night
questions.explanation: Pedestrians are most common at intersections and crosswalks, where drivers must yield to them.

questions.prompt: In Arizona, an adult 21 or older is legally impaired for DUI at a blood alcohol concentration of:
choices: 0.08 percent or higher
0.10 percent or higher
0.05 percent or higher
0.12 percent or higher
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older can be charged with DUI at a BAC of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: For a driver under 21 in Arizona, the legal limit for alcohol while driving is:
choices: Zero, any amount is illegal
0.08 percent
0.05 percent
0.02 percent
questions.explanation: Arizona has zero tolerance for drivers under 21, so any alcohol in the body while driving is unlawful.

questions.prompt: In Arizona, using a handheld cell phone while driving is:
choices: Illegal; only hands-free use is allowed
Always legal
Legal at low speeds
Legal if you stop at red lights
questions.explanation: Arizona prohibits holding a cell phone while driving, allowing only hands-free use.

questions.prompt: The only thing that will sober up a person who has been drinking is:
choices: Time
Coffee
A cold shower
Fresh air
questions.explanation: Only the passage of time removes alcohol from the body; coffee and other tricks do not work.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving in Arizona is:
choices: A primary offense for which you can be stopped
Allowed if you are careful
Only illegal for teens
A secondary offense only
questions.explanation: Texting while driving is a primary offense, meaning an officer can stop you for that alone.

questions.prompt: The BAC limit for operating a commercial vehicle in Arizona is:
choices: 0.04 percent
0.08 percent
0.02 percent
0.10 percent
questions.explanation: Commercial drivers can be charged with DUI at a lower BAC of 0.04 percent.

questions.prompt: Besides alcohol, what can impair your ability to drive safely?
choices: Prescription and over-the-counter medications
Wearing a seat belt
Driving at the speed limit
Using headlights at night
questions.explanation: Many prescription and over-the-counter drugs can impair driving by affecting alertness and coordination.

questions.prompt: The safest choice if you plan to drink alcohol is to:
choices: Arrange a sober ride in advance
Drink only beer
Wait one hour before driving
Drive slowly afterward
questions.explanation: Planning a sober ride is the safest way to avoid impaired driving.

questions.prompt: Even if your BAC is below 0.08, you can still be charged with DUI in Arizona if you are:
choices: Impaired to the slightest degree
Driving at night
Over 21
Using a hands-free phone
questions.explanation: Arizona allows a DUI charge whenever a driver is impaired to the slightest degree, even below 0.08.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect a driver?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
It improves concentration
It sharpens vision
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reaction time and impairs judgment, vision, and coordination, making driving unsafe.

questions.prompt: If you are caught in a dust storm (haboob) in Arizona, you should:
choices: Pull off the road, stop, and turn off your lights
Speed up to get through it
Slow down but keep driving with lights on
Stop in your lane and wait
questions.explanation: The 'Pull Aside, Stay Alive' rule says to pull off the road, stop, and turn off your lights so others do not follow you off the road.

questions.prompt: In Arizona, you must turn on your headlights:
choices: From sunset to sunrise and when visibility is poor
Only on highways
Only when it is raining
Only in tunnels
questions.explanation: Headlights are required from sunset to sunrise and whenever conditions like rain or dust reduce visibility.

questions.prompt: In fog or blowing dust, you should use:
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while moving at full speed
questions.explanation: Low beams reduce glare in fog and dust, while high beams reflect back and worsen visibility.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Brake hard immediately
Turn the wheel sharply opposite the skid
Accelerate to regain control
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering gently where you want to go helps you recover from a skid.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning occurs when:
choices: Tires lose contact with the road on a film of water
The engine overheats
Brakes get too cold
You drive too slowly in rain
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning happens when tires ride on top of water and lose traction with the road surface.

questions.prompt: When driving on wet roads, you should:
choices: Reduce speed and avoid sudden braking
Drive at the normal speed limit
Brake hard at every turn
Use cruise control
questions.explanation: Slowing down and braking gently on wet roads reduces the chance of skidding or hydroplaning.

questions.prompt: In poor visibility conditions, you should adjust your driving by:
choices: Slowing down and increasing following distance
Speeding up to leave the area
Following more closely to see better
Turning off your lights
questions.explanation: Reduced visibility requires slower speeds and a larger following distance to drive safely.

questions.prompt: When you stop during a dust storm, you should take your foot off the brake so that:
choices: Your brake lights do not lead other drivers off the road
The car can roll forward
The engine cools faster
You save fuel
questions.explanation: Turning off lights and releasing the brake prevents other drivers from following your taillights off the road.

questions.prompt: Why should you slow down at night?
choices: Your headlights limit how far ahead you can see
Speed limits are higher at night
Roads are always wet at night
Other drivers cannot see you anyway
questions.explanation: At night you should slow down because you can only see as far as your headlights reach.

questions.prompt: Where is parking prohibited?
choices: In front of a fire hydrant
In a marked parking space
In a driveway you own
In a parking garage
questions.explanation: Parking in front of a fire hydrant is illegal because it can block emergency access.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill keeps the vehicle from rolling into traffic.

questions.prompt: In Arizona, who is required to wear a seat belt?
choices: The driver and front-seat passengers
Only the driver
Only children
No one over 18
questions.explanation: Arizona law requires the driver and front-seat passengers to wear seat belts.

questions.prompt: Young children must ride in:
choices: An appropriate child restraint or booster seat
The front seat with a lap belt
Any seat without restraint
The driver's lap
questions.explanation: Arizona law requires young children to be secured in a child restraint or booster appropriate for their age and size.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space reserved for people with disabilities only if:
choices: You have a valid disability permit or plate
You will be quick
No other spaces are open
It is after business hours
questions.explanation: Disability parking spaces may be used only by vehicles displaying a valid disability permit or plate.

questions.prompt: Before leaving a parked position at a curb, you should:
choices: Signal and check for traffic and pedestrians
Pull out quickly
Honk twice
Wait for someone to wave you out
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking for traffic and pedestrians before pulling out prevents collisions.

questions.prompt: When you park your vehicle, you should always:
choices: Set the parking brake
Leave the engine running
Leave the doors unlocked
Leave the keys in the ignition
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake helps keep a parked vehicle from rolling, especially on a slope.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to park:
choices: In a crosswalk or intersection
In a metered space
In your own garage
In a marked lot
questions.explanation: Parking in a crosswalk or intersection is prohibited because it blocks pedestrians and traffic.

questions.prompt: When should you make sure everyone in the vehicle is buckled?
choices: Before you start moving
Only on the highway
After you reach the speed limit
Only when police are nearby
questions.explanation: Everyone should be properly buckled before the vehicle begins to move.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a graduated instruction (learner's) permit in Arizona?
choices: 15 years and 6 months
16 years
15 years
14 years and 6 months
questions.explanation: Arizona teens may apply for a graduated instruction permit at age 15 years and 6 months.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving practice must a teen complete before a Class G license?
choices: At least 30 hours, including 10 at night
At least 10 hours
At least 50 hours
No supervised hours are required
questions.explanation: Teens must complete at least 30 hours of supervised practice, including 10 hours at night, before licensing.

questions.prompt: While holding an instruction permit, a teen must drive with:
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 in the front passenger seat
Any passenger over 16
No supervision after dark
Another permit holder
questions.explanation: Permit holders must always be accompanied by a licensed driver at least 21 years old in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: How long must a teen hold the instruction permit before applying for a Class G license?
choices: At least 6 months
At least 1 month
At least 1 year
No waiting period
questions.explanation: The instruction permit must be held for at least 6 months before applying for a graduated driver license.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Arizona written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: The Arizona written knowledge test contains 30 questions.

questions.prompt: What score is required to pass the Arizona written knowledge test?
choices: 80 percent
70 percent
60 percent
90 percent
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the questions correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: At what age can a teen apply for a Class G graduated driver license in Arizona?
questions.explanation: A teen may apply for a Class G graduated driver license at age 16 after meeting the permit and practice requirements.

questions.prompt: Besides the written test, what else is required to get an instruction permit?
choices: A vision screening
A road test
A motorcycle endorsement
A commercial license
questions.explanation: Applicants must pass a vision screening in addition to the written knowledge test to get a permit.

questions.prompt: The questions on the Arizona knowledge test are drawn mainly from:
choices: The Arizona Driver License Manual
A national driving website
Insurance company guides
Word of mouth
questions.explanation: Test questions come from the Arizona Driver License Manual, so studying it is the best preparation.

questions.prompt: During the first 6 months with a Class G license, a teen generally may not drive between:
choices: Midnight and 5 a.m.
10 p.m. and 6 a.m.
9 p.m. and 5 a.m.
11 p.m. and 4 a.m.
questions.explanation: For the first 6 months, Class G drivers generally cannot drive between midnight and 5 a.m. except for limited reasons.

questions.prompt: During the first 6 months with a Class G license, a teen may carry no more than:
choices: One passenger under 18 who is not family
Three passengers under 18
No passengers at all
Two passengers over 16
questions.explanation: For the first 6 months, a Class G driver may not carry more than one non-family passenger under 18.

questions.prompt: Arizona's implied consent law means that by driving you agree to:
choices: Submit to alcohol or drug testing if lawfully arrested for DUI
Pay all traffic tickets
Allow vehicle searches anytime
Give up your license on request
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, drivers agree to chemical testing if lawfully arrested for DUI.

questions.prompt: Refusing a chemical test after a DUI arrest in Arizona results in:
choices: Suspension of your driving privilege
A warning only
No penalty
A reduced charge
questions.explanation: Refusing the chemical test leads to suspension of your driving privilege under the implied consent law.

questions.prompt: Accumulating too many points on your driving record can lead to:
choices: License suspension or required traffic survival school
A higher speed limit
Automatic license renewal
A free defensive driving course
questions.explanation: Too many points can result in license suspension or a requirement to attend traffic survival school.

questions.prompt: A DUI conviction in Arizona can include:
choices: Fines, license suspension, jail, and an ignition interlock
Only a warning
A point reduction
A free ride home
questions.explanation: DUI convictions carry serious penalties including fines, suspension, jail time, and an ignition interlock device.

questions.prompt: An exception that allows a Class G driver to drive after midnight during the first 6 months is:
choices: Driving to or from work or school
Driving with friends
Driving for fun
Driving on weekends
questions.explanation: Driving to or from work, school, or a religious activity is an allowed exception to the nighttime restriction.

questions.prompt: For drivers under 18, committing certain traffic violations can:
choices: Extend the graduated license restrictions
Remove all restrictions
Add bonus driving privileges
Have no consequences
questions.explanation: Traffic violations by drivers under 18 can extend the graduated driver license restriction period.

questions.prompt: The nighttime restriction for a Class G driver does not apply when:
choices: Accompanied by a parent or guardian in the front seat
Driving alone slowly
Driving on city streets
Driving under the speed limit
questions.explanation: The nighttime restriction does not apply when a parent or legal guardian rides in the front passenger seat.
